golden_categories.name: Standard FQDN Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates fully qualified domain names (FQDN) with default options.

golden_categories.name: Trailing Dot Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow a trailing dot.

golden_categories.name: No TLD Requirement
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs without requiring a top-level domain (TLD).

golden_categories.name: Numeric TLDs Without Requirement
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with numeric TLDs when TLD requirement is disabled.

golden_categories.name: Wildcard Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow wildcard domains.

golden_categories.name: Combined Options
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with multiple options enabled: trailing dot, underscores, and numeric TLDs.

generated_categories.name: Basic Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests that ensure the input adheres to fundamental FQDN rules, such as being a string and rejecting invalid characters or formats.

generated_categories.name: Trailing Dot Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests related to the handling of trailing dots in FQDNs, depending on the `allow_trailing_dot` option.

generated_categories.name: Wildcard Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests that validate the behavior when wildcards are allowed in FQDNs using the `allow_wildcard` option.

generated_categories.name: TLD Requirements
generated_categories.description: Tests that ensure proper handling of top-level domains (TLDs), including their presence and format, based on the `require_tld` option.

generated_categories.name: Numeric TLD Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests that validate the acceptance or rejection of numeric TLDs based on the `allow_numeric_tld` option.

generated_categories.name: Length Constraints
generated_categories.description: Tests that verify the enforcement of length constraints on FQDN parts, such as the maximum length of labels.

generated_categories.name: Underscore Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests that validate the behavior when underscores are allowed or disallowed in FQDNs using the `allow_underscores` option.

golden_to_generated.reason: Both categories focus on fundamental FQDN validation, including rejecting invalid formats and characters.
Both categories address the handling of trailing dots in FQDNs with the `allow_trailing_dot` option.
GC-3 focuses on disabling TLD requirements, while GENC-4 includes tests for TLD presence and format, partially overlapping with GC-3.
Both categories focus on validating numeric TLDs when the `allow_numeric_tld` option is enabled.
Both categories address wildcard handling in FQDNs using the `allow_wildcard` option.
GC-6 combines multiple options (trailing dot, numeric TLDs, underscores), which are fragmented across GENC-2, GENC-5, and GENC-7.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 aligns with GC-1 in validating fundamental FQDN rules and rejecting invalid formats.
GENC-2 matches GC-2 for trailing dot handling and partially overlaps with GC-6, which combines multiple options including trailing dots.
GENC-3 and GC-5 both focus on wildcard handling in FQDNs.
GENC-4 partially overlaps with GC-3 by addressing TLD requirements, though GC-3 specifically disables the requirement.
GENC-5 strongly matches GC-4 for numeric TLD handling and partially overlaps with GC-6, which combines multiple options including numeric TLDs.
GENC-6 focuses on length constraints, which is not covered by any golden category.
GENC-7 addresses underscore handling, which is one of the options combined in GC-6.

novel_generated_categories.reason: GENC-6 introduces tests for length constraints, which are not addressed by any golden category.
